Field.tooltip: Select the source for the brightness of this device.
Field.Label: Brightness Source:

Field.tooltip: Enter the brightness level the device should be set to. Range is 0 to 100 percent.
Field.Label: Brightness (0-100):
Field.Description: Brightness level the device should be set to.

Field.tooltip: Select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action is executed should be used as the brightness level for this device. If the variable contains any text other than a number from 0 to 100, nothing will happen and an error will be displayed in the log.
Field.Label: Brightness Variable:

Field.tooltip: Select another Indigo dimmer device whose brightness at the time this action is executed should be used to set the brightness of this device.
Field.Label: Other Dimmer:

Field.tooltip: Check this box to select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action executes should be used as the ramp rate for this device.
Field.Label: Use Ramp Rate Variable

Field.tooltip: Enter the amount of time, in seconds, it should take for this device to change to the new setting. Precission up to 0.1 seconds is supported.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate (0-540):

Field.tooltip: Select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action executes should be used as the number of seconds for this device to change to the new brightness level.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate Variable:
Ramp Rates represent the full time needed to transition from one brightness level to the next and can be specified in increments of 0.1 seconds. Range: 0 to 540 sec.

NOTE: Hue light bulbs are not capable of displaying the same full spectrum of light that your computer monitor can display. Thus, when setting Red, Green, and Blue values, the bulb will switch to the closest color it can render to the color you specified.* Other supported color lights (LightStrips, LivingColors, etc.) have a wider color range and may represent certain colors (such as green) better.

Field.tooltip: Click to pick a color.
Field.Label: Color:

Field.tooltip: Enter the red component level. Range is 0 to 255.
Field.Label: Red:
(0 - 255) 0 = red LEDs off 255 = red LEDs on full

Field.tooltip: Enter the green component level. Range is 0 to 255.
Field.Label: Green:
(0 - 255) 0 = green LEDs off 255 = green LEDs on full

Field.tooltip: Enter the blue component level. Range is 0 to 255.
Field.Label: Blue:
(0 - 255) 0 = blue LEDs off 255 = blue LEDs on full

Field.tooltip: Check this box to select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action executes should be used as the ramp rate for this device.
Field.Label: Use Ramp Rate Variable

Field.tooltip: Enter the amount of time, in seconds, it should take for this device to change to the new setting. Precission up to 0.1 seconds is supported.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate (0-540):

Field.tooltip: Select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action executes should be used as the number of seconds for this device to change to the new setting.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate Variable:
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant. Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time), accurate to 0.1 seconds.

* Within 10 seconds (or less) after this action executes, the device states will update to reflect a more accurate representation of the color being presented by the Hue device. These conversions are done by the Hue Bridge itself.

NOTE: Many "full color" lights are not capable of displaying the same full spectrum of light that your computer monitor can display. Thus, when setting Hue and Saturation values, the light will switch to the closest color it can render to the color you specified.*

Field.tooltip: Click to pick a color.
Field.Label: Color:

Field.tooltip: Enter the hue value. Range is 0 to 360 degrees.
Field.Label: Hue:
(0 - 360°) 0 (and 360) = red, 60 = yellow, 120 = green, 180 = cyan, 240 = blue, 300 = magenta.

Field.tooltip: Enter the saturation value. Range is 0 to 100 percent.
Field.Label: Saturation:
(0 - 100%) 0 = white (Hue is meaningless). 100 = full color (no white mixed in).

Field.tooltip: Select the source for the brightness of this device.
Field.Label: Brightness Source:

Field.tooltip: Enter the brightness level the device should be set to. Range is 0 to 100 percent.
Field.Label: Brightness (0-100):
Field.Description: Brightness level the device should be set to.

Field.tooltip: Select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action is executed should be used as the brightness level for this device. If the variable contains any text other than a number from 0 to 100, nothing will happen and an error will be displayed in the log.
Field.Label: Brightness Variable:

Field.tooltip: Select another Indigo dimmer device whose brightness at the time this action is executed should be used to set the brightness of this device.
Field.Label: Other Dimmer:

Field.tooltip: Check this box to select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action executes should be used as the ramp rate for this device.
Field.Label: Use Ramp Rate Variable

Field.tooltip: Enter the amount of time, in seconds, it should take for this device to change to the new setting. Precission up to 0.1 seconds is supported.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate (0-540):

Field.tooltip: Select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action executes should be used as the number of seconds for this device to change to the new setting.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate Variable:
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant. Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time), accurate to 0.1 seconds.

* Within 10 seconds (or less) after this action executes, the device states, including the hue and saturation, will update to reflect a more accurate representation of the color being presented by the Hue device. These conversions are done by the Hue Bridge itself.
NOTE: Each light device has it's own colorimetry range (gamut). Entering values outside a device's range can result in unexpected colors.

Field.tooltip: Enter the x chromatisity component value. Range is 0 to 1.0.
Field.Label: x (X Chromatisety):

Field.tooltip: Enter the y chromatisity component value. Range is 0 to 1.0.
Field.Label: y (Y Chromatisety):

Field.tooltip: Enter the Y luminosity component value. Range is 0 to 1.0.
Field.Label: Y (Luminosity):

Field.tooltip: Check this box to select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action executes should be used as the ramp rate for this device.
Field.Label: Use Ramp Rate Variable

Field.tooltip: Enter the amount of time, in seconds, it should take for this device to change to the new setting. Precission up to 0.1 seconds is supported.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate (0-540):

Field.tooltip: Select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action executes should be used as the number of seconds for this device to change to the new setting.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate Variable:
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant. Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time), accurate to 0.1 seconds.

Color temperature represents the shade of white being seen. When entering a Custom Temperature, a lower number means a "warmer", or more orange shade of white. A higher number means a "cooler", or more blue shade of white.

Field.tooltip: Select a color temperature preset or select Custom Teperature/Brightness to enter a custom color temperature and brightness value. Presets are based on those provided by Philips for Hue.
Field.Label: Preset Color Recipes:
Select a "Preset Color Recipe" or select "Custom Temperature/Brightness" to enter a specific color temperature (in Kelvin).

Field.tooltip: Select how you'd like to specify a color temperature setting. Either by entering a color temperature or by specifying an Indigo variable that contains the color temperature value.
Field.Label: Temperature Source:

Field.tooltip: Enter a color temperature value in Kelvin. Range is 2000 to 6500, and only applies to Hue devices that support the use of color temperature.
Field.Label: Color Temperature:

Field.tooltip: Select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action is executed will be used as the color temperature setting in Kelvin. The variable must have a value in the range of 2000 to 6500.
Field.Label: Color Temperature Variable:
(2000 - 6500 K) 2000 = very warm (more orange). 6500 = very cool (more blue).

Field.tooltip: Select the source for the brightness of this device.
Field.Label: Brightness Source:

Field.tooltip: Enter the brightness level the device should be set to. Range is 0 to 100 percent.
Field.Label: Brightness (0-100):
Field.Description: Brightness level the device should be set to.

Field.tooltip: Select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action is executed should be used as the brightness level for this device. If the variable contains any text other than a number from 0 to 100, nothing will happen and an error will be displayed in the log.
Field.Label: Brightness Variable:

Field.tooltip: Select another Indigo dimmer device whose brightness at the time this action is executed should be used to set the brightness of this device.
Field.Label: Other Dimmer:

Field.tooltip: Check this box to select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action executes should be used as the ramp rate for this device.
Field.Label: Use Ramp Rate Variable

Field.tooltip: Enter the amount of time, in seconds, it should take for this device to change to the new setting. Precission up to 0.1 seconds is supported.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate (0-540):

Field.tooltip: Select an Indigo variable whose value at the time this action executes should be used as the number of seconds for this device to change to the new brightness level.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate Variable:
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant. Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time), accurate to 0.1 seconds.

* Within 10 seconds (or less) after this action executes, the device states, including the color temperature, will update to reflect a more accurate representation of the color being presented by the Hue bulb. These conversions are done by the Hue Bridge itself.
Hue Sensors hav the option to set senbsitivity

Field.tooltip: Select the Gateway this preset is associated with
Field.Label: Hue Bridge number:

Field.tooltip: Select a preset effect. Effect presets are usually built into the light device itself. These presets are not the same as Hue Lights presets.
Field.Label: Presets:

Select a Hue device effect. This feature requires the latest Hue Bridge firmware. The Hue lighting devices have only ever offered the "Color Loop" effect which cycles through all hues at the current saturation and brightness level.
light device to send the command to:
scene to send the command to:

Field.tooltip: Select a preset effect. Effect presets are usually built into the light device itself. These presets are not the same as Hue Lights presets.
Field.Label: Presets:

Field.tooltip: Select the Preset in which the device's current settings should be stored. Presets with an asterisk (*) next to them have data already saved in them. Selecting one of these Presets will overwrite the data saved in that Preset.
Field.Label: Preset:

Select the Preset in which the device's current settings should be stored. Presets with an asterisk (*) next to them have data already saved in them. Selecting one of these Presets will overwrite the data saved in that Preset.

Field.tooltip: Give the Preset a name (or change the current name).
Field.Label: Preset Name:
Optionally assign a name to this Preset.

Field.tooltip: Enter the amount of time, in seconds, it should take for this device to change to the new setting. Precission up to 0.1 seconds is supported.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate (0-540):
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant Save a Ramp Rate (transition time) to be used when recalling this Preset, accurate to 0.1 seconds.

Field.tooltip: Select the Preset that should be applied to the device. Presets with an asterisk (*) next to them have data saved in them.
Field.Label: Preset:
Select the Preset that should be applied to the device. Presets with an asterisk (*) next to them have data saved in them.

Field.tooltip: Enter the amount of time, in seconds, it should take for this device to change to the new setting. Precission up to 0.1 seconds is supported.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate (0-540):
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant Optionally override this Preset's saved Ramp Rate (transition time). This overrides the device's default rate as well. Ramp Rates are accurate to 0.1 seconds.
With this action you can enable or disable a sensor device on the bridge.

Field.tooltip: Select a Hue Sensor.
Field.Label: Hue Sensor (Name on bridge):
Hue Scenes are stored on the Hue Bridge and can be activated (for pro bridge only).
the plugin reads all scenes from the bridge and here you can select one and send activation (recall) of that scene to the bridge
Speed only applies to dynamic scenes. Has no effect on static scenes.
Set what a light does when physically power-cycled (wall switch off/on). Only individual lights are supported, not groups.

List.Option: Restore if was on, else off

Field.Label: Brightness % (1-100, used when "Set a fixed value"):
Color temp mirek (153=cool, 500=warm, used when "Set color temperature"):
Start a sunrise wake-up simulation. The bridge manages the transition autonomously. Requires a newer Hue bulb that supports timed effects.
Smart scenes adapt brightness and color temperature to the time of day and natural light cycle. They are stored per room or zone on the bridge.
Hue Scenes are stored on the Hue Bridge and are organized here by which application created them.

Field.tooltip: Select the Hue Bridge with which this Scene is associated
Field.Label: Hue Bridge Number:

Field.tooltip: Scene Creators are the applications on your devices that have connected to the Hue Bridge and created Scenes.
Field.Label: Scene Creator:
Select a Scene Creator from the Hue Bridge first (or use "All Scene Creators").

Field.tooltip: Scenes are stored on the Hue Bridge along with which application created them. Scenes in this menu were created by the Scene Creator selected above.
Field.Label: Scene:
Some Scenes may have the same name because of how the Hue app creates them on the Bridge. Use the Lights Affected list below to distinguish between Scenes with the same name.

Field.tooltip: The Hue Bridge allows you to limit a Scene to just one Group, as long as at least one light in the Scene is part of the Group. Lights that are part of a Scene but NOT part of the selected Group Limit will be unaffected.
Field.Label: Group Limit:
Limit the Scene to a specific Hue Group.

Field.tooltip: This list shows all the lights that will be affected by the combination of the Scene and Group Limit selections above. These cannot be edited.
Field.Label: Lights Affected:
This list shows which lights are affected by the Scene and Group Limit selections above. This cannot be edited.
